titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paula Vogel Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 6, 2020 at 03:41

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

bioghist.head: Paula Vogel
bioghist.p: Paula Vogel (b. 1951) is an American playwright, university professor, and advocate for gay and lesbian rights. She won the 1998 Pulitzer Prize for Drama for her play How I Learned to Drive.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 38 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 37, 39, 40, 51, 52 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Digital files (student records): Restricted until 2095. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Digital files (financial records): Restricted until 2065. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paula Vogel Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Paula Vogel on the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2014.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paula Vogel Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, family papers, teaching materials, photographs, posters, awards, audiovisual materials, electronic media, and other papers documenting the life and work of playwright Paula Vogel. Writings include scripts, drafts, production materials, research materials, clippings, and other papers relating to plays by Vogel including Hot and Throbbing, How I Learned to Drive, Civil War Christmas, The Mineola Twins, and others.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Writings, 1970-2009. II. Correspondence and personal papers, 1949-2013. III. Teaching materials, 1970-2009. IV. Photographs and slides, circa 1970s-2013. V. Posters, 1970-2009. VI. Awards and recognitions, 1990-2009. VII. Audiovisual materials, circa 1990s. VIII. Electronic media, circa 2000s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Writings are arranged in roughly the order in which they were received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings include scripts, drafts, production materials, research materials, clippings, and other papers related to plays and screenplays written by Paula Vogel. Notable works include Hot and Throbbing, How I Learned to Drive, Civil War Christmas, and The Mineola Twins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following plays: Balls, Cried the Queen; Meg; Dorr; Silver Threads; The Swann Song of Sir Henry; Apple Brown Betty; And Baby Makes Seven; and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes The Oldest Profession and I Am No Angel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles; Golden Girls - Hold the Mayo; The Unmasking of O; The Oldest Profession; Teaser; and others.

did.unittitle: The Oldest Profession, Hot and Throbbing, The Baltimore Waltz, How I Learned to Drive
Signature Theater and Arena Stage productions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes scripts, playbills, programs, and press materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes playbills, programs, press, contact sheets.

did.unittitle: Clippings, photographs and production materials from various productions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resumes, student evaluations and other materials.

did.unittitle: Photograph, Academy of Arts and Letters award ceremony
Present "On the occasion of her first Pulitzer Prize"
New York Drama Critics' Circle award for best play How I Learned to Drive
How I Learned to Drive, Arena Stage
Magazine pages from "Out", article Paula Vogel in High Gear.
How I Learned to Drive, Intiman Theatre
Cartoon, larger version of "On the occation of her first Pulitzer" gift
